symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden sharp localised right anterior neck pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemorrhage into a benign thyroid cyst/nodule
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden capsular stretch from intracystic bleeding produces instantaneous sharp pain.
Malignancy typically grows gradually and painlessly, not instantaneously with acute pain.
Thyroiditis pain builds over days post-viral rather than in an instant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly appearing discrete anterior neck lump

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemorrhage into a benign thyroid cyst/nodule
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid distension of a pre-existing cyst by blood produces a fast-appearing discrete mass.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Expanding cervical haematoma with airway threat
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Any rapidly expanding anterior neck mass must be screened for airway compromise.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemorrhage into a benign thyroid cyst/nodule
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mass effect of the distended nodule on the pharynx/oesophagus produces globus and mild dysphagia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemorrhage into a benign thyroid cyst/nodule
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Movement with deglutition localises the mass to the thyroid/larynx (invested in pretracheal fascia).
A lateral branchial cleft lesion does not move upward with swallowing the way a thyroid lesion does.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fluctuant, mobile, non-fixed, moderately tender ~3 cm right thyroid-lobe mass on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemorrhage into a benign thyroid cyst/nodule
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fluctuance (fluid/blood), mobility and lack of fixation are reassuring benign features — but this is a clinician palpation finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain.
Malignant nodes are typically hard and fixed rather than fluctuant and mobile.
Absence of pathological cervical nodes lowers malignancy concern — a clinician examination finding, unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear airway without stridor on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Expanding cervical haematoma with airway threat
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patent airway with no stridor is reassuring and is what keeps the tier below emergency — but airway auscultation/assessment is a clinician finding, not a telehealth one.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal overlying skin (no erythema or warmth) on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of skin erythema and warmth argues strongly against an infective/abscess process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with normal vital signs (SpO2 99% room air, HR 85, RR 16, T 36.8 C)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever and normal observations argue against infection and against current airway/respiratory compromise — clinician-measured vitals, sealed and not available to the AI Doctor over chat.
Absence of hoarseness makes recurrent laryngeal nerve involvement (which would raise malignancy concern) less likely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Expanding cervical haematoma with airway threat
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Comfortable breathing self-reported is reassuring but does not exclude progression — must be safety-netted.
No thyrotoxic prodrome makes thyroiditis less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No neck irradiation and no family history of thyroid cancer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the two main historical risk factors lowers the pre-test probability of thyroid carcinoma.

investigation_details.expected_result: Discrete cystic thyroid lesion with internal haemorrhage; enables sonographic malignancy risk stratification
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without ultrasound the haemorrhagic cyst is unconfirmed and malignancy is not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute haemorrhage into a benign thyroid cyst/nodule
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ultrasound is the first-line confirmatory investigation and the tool that formally down-ranks malignancy — never available in real time over telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute haemorrhage into a benign thyroid cyst/nodule
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden sharp pain + rapidly appearing discrete mass + globus + movement with swallowing together point strongly to acute haemorrhage into a thyroid nodule.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line imaging to confirm a haemorrhagic cyst and risk-stratify for malignancy; brought forward to urgent because the AI cannot examine the neck or airway.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical assessment of the neck mass and airway
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Palpation of the mass and airway assessment cannot be done over telehealth and are needed the same day to exclude an expanding haematoma.
Baseline thyroid function to screen for coexisting dysfunction; not time-critical.
Considered after ultrasound risk stratification for drainage or cytology; not a first-line telehealth action and not to be ordered before imaging.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT neck with contrast as a first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not the first-line investigation for a thyroid nodule; ultrasound is preferred first, and iodinated contrast can complicate later thyroid workup.
